title: No占퐎a
content:
혻혻혻혻No占퐎a, when sad Fall
혻혻혻혻Has grayed the fallow;
혻혻혻혻Leaf-cramped the wood-brook's brawl
혻혻혻혻In pool and shallow;
혻혻혻혻When, by the woodside, tall
혻혻혻혻Stands sere the mallow.

혻혻혻혻No占퐎a, when gray gold
혻혻혻혻And golden gray
혻혻혻혻The crackling hollows fold
혻혻혻혻By every way,
혻혻혻혻Shall I thy face behold,
혻혻혻혻Dear bit of May?

혻혻혻혻When webs are cribs for dew,
혻혻혻혻And gossamers
혻혻혻혻Streak by you, silver-blue;
혻혻혻혻When silence stirs
혻혻혻혻One leaf, of rusty hue,
혻혻혻혻Among the burrs:

혻혻혻혻No占퐎a, through the wood,
혻혻혻혻Or through the grain,
혻혻혻혻Come, with the hoiden mood
혻혻혻혻Of wind and rain
혻혻혻혻Fresh in thy sunny blood,
혻혻혻혻Sweetheart, again.

혻혻혻혻No占퐎a, when the corn,
혻혻혻혻Reaped on the fields,
혻혻혻혻The asters' stars adorn;
혻혻혻혻And purple shields
혻혻혻혻Of ironweeds lie torn
혻혻혻혻Among the wealds:

혻혻혻혻No占퐎a, haply then,
혻혻혻혻Thou being with me,
혻혻혻혻Each ruined greenwood glen
혻혻혻혻Will bud and be
혻혻혻혻Spring's with the spring again,
혻혻혻혻The spring in thee.

혻혻혻혻Thou of the breezy tread;
혻혻혻혻Feet of the breeze:
혻혻혻혻Thou of the sunbeam head;
혻혻혻혻Heart like a bee's:
혻혻혻혻Face like a woodland-bred
혻혻혻혻Anemone's.

혻혻혻혻Thou to October bring
혻혻혻혻An April part!
혻혻혻혻Come! make the wild birds sing,
혻혻혻혻The blossoms start!
혻혻혻혻No占퐎a, with the spring
혻혻혻혻Wild in thy heart!

혻혻혻혻Come with our golden year:
혻혻혻혻Come as its gold:
혻혻혻혻With the same laughing, clear,
혻혻혻혻Loved voice of old:
혻혻혻혻In thy cool hair one dear
혻혻혻혻Wild marigold.
meaning:
"No占퐎a" is a quintessential example of Madison Julius Cawein's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
